NEG. ROUNDS

TOPOLOGY
RIGHT OF WAY
An agent-to-agent right-of-way protocol for physical swarms — demoed in orbit
DROP TIMELINE JSON TO REPLACE FIXTURE
ALL CLEAR
SCENE LAYERS
SATELLITE COMMS — A2A CHANNEL

RIGHT OF WAY

An agent-to-agent right-of-way protocol for physical swarms — demoed in orbit

Two satellites are about to pass dangerously close. Each has an AI agent. They negotiate who dodges — in plain English — and a physics engine referees every deal. There's no air-traffic control in orbit; in real life this is coordinated by email (ESA vs Starlink-44, Sept 2019). Press ▶ to watch.

WAIT — SATELLITES HAVE FUEL?

Yes: a small tank of propellant for tiny thrusters. Firing them is a burn; the lifetime supply is Δv (delta-v), measured in m/s. Fuel is mission lifetime — every dodge costs months of operational life, which is why nobody wants to move and this is a negotiation, not a calculation. A dead satellite (or an empty tank) physically can't move — someone else must.

WHO HAS RIGHT OF WAY?

Honestly: no agreed standard exists — that's the gap this project pokes at. The demo models it as a priority number (crewed craft > science mission > constellation sat #4,412 > debris); the norm says the lower priority dodges. The interesting moments are when the norm can't hold — no fuel, or an operator who won't answer — and the agents must talk it out.

THE PHYSICS REFEREE

Agents can argue, bargain, even lie — no deal counts until a deterministic orbital-mechanics engine confirms the burn clears the encounter and fits the fuel budget. Every burn triggers a re-scan (a dodge that creates a new near-miss sends everyone back to the table), and claims are audited: say "I can't move" with a full tank, and the deal is voided — the burn lands on you.

READING THE SCREEN

Red dots + line = the pair at risk and their predicted close pass. Orange arrow = the dodge burn. Green = safe. STORY pauses the orbits while each message plays. Other runs: the lying satellite · a real conjunction this week · the synthetic proof.

MIT · github.com/Dunya-8a/right-of-way · PROTOCOL.md (the same loop works for drones, ships — any fleet with no central boss)

collision risk predicted close pass (+ distance) dodge burn clear
T+00:00
